title: Does the cyclic group C_963 admit a (963,222,51)-difference set?
lean_gate_reason: The packet is human-ready on bounded audit and the proof artifacts are preserved. Lean is now a secondary archival seal for an already publishable exact slice, not a prerequisite for publication status.
micro_paper_assessment: The exact named cyclic row now has a short structural nonexistence proof anchored to the [9,107] quotient, and publication audit did not uncover rediscovery within budget. After correcting the citation from a blanket multiplier claim to the sufficient 107-multiplier theorem, this is strong enough for the HUMAN_READY micro-paper lane.
family_affinity: high: the proof is tied directly to the family-defining [9,107] quotient structure
generalization_signal: moderate: the orbit-count contradiction template may transfer to other isolated [m,q] rows with a half-order multiplier on the prime quotient
strongest_honest_claim: The cyclic group C_963 does not admit a (963,222,51)-difference set; a short quotient-orbit obstruction on the C_107 contraction closes the exact [9,107] row.
paper_title_hint: Nonexistence of a cyclic (963,222,51) difference set
candidate_theorem_slice: For a cyclic group of order m q with q prime, if a w-multiplier forces a prime-quotient contraction to be constant on one fixed point and equal nonzero orbits of size h, then the congruence k = a_0 + h M can force nonexistence when the kernel-size bound makes a_0 incompatible with k mod h.
